Emi Buendia will push for a move to Arsenal if they make a formal attempt to sign him this month, according to a report.

James Benge reports for CBS Sports that Emi Buendia is prepared to push for a move to Arsenal if they try to sign him this month. They’ve already held informal talks with the Norwich City man over a possible January transfer.

This could be important, as Norwich are currently planning to ignore any and all offers for Buendia in the winter window. Sky Sports claim Norwich they’ll ignore even lucrative offers for him.

Daniel Farke told the media: “He is our player and will continue to be our player. He is on a long-term contract so we keep him.”

Buendia has seven goals and seven assists in 19 games, leading Norwich City up to top spot in the Championship table. As things stand, he has them on course for promotion.

It’s no real surprise they don’t want to sell him mid-season in those circumstances.

But if the player really pushes his club to let him go, that might change the picture. They might worry about the effect of forcing an unsettled player to stay, and consider cashing in to reinvest in a replacement.

Prior to the window opening, there were suggestions Norwich were planning for this eventuality. They were reportedly looking at potential replacements just in case a bid came in.

Right now, it looks like we’re all just waiting to find out whether or not Arsenal will make that bid and set everything in motion.

ARSENAL SHOULD PROMOTE OKONKWO WITH MACEY SET TO LEAVE ON LOAN

With Matt Macey reportedly set to join Hibernian on loan until the end of the season, it appears that an opportunity could open up for another goalkeeper to become Arsenal’s third-choice in that position.

It is of course possible that Arsenal could look to bring in a shot-stopper from elsewhere, and there may well be suggestions that they should sign somebody as a second-choice given concerns over Alex Runarsson.

However, Arsenal have a very talented goalkeeper in their academy who is capable of stepping up to be third choice.

Arthur Okonkwo has long been seen as a potential future member of the first-team squad, and this may be the opportunity that he has been waiting for.

The 19-year-old trained with the first-team squad yesterday and has impressed on the few occasions that he has been called upon at youth level this season.

Karl Hein, who is already a senior Estonia international, has often been selected ahead of Okonkwo for Arsenal’s U23 side.

While Hein has excellent reflexes and possesses much in the way of potential, he does have a tendency to make costly errors at times.

Okonkwo, in contrast, is a much more reliable goalkeeper at this stage and is a calming presence between the sticks.

Last season was incredibly tough for Okonkwo as he missed the entire campaign due to a health problem and this season he was left out for several games following a disciplinary issue.

Now, though, he appears to be back on track and certainly seems to be Arsenal’s best option to step in if Macey does go on loan.

Why Arsenal are interested in Norwich star Emi Buendia

Arsenal are keen on signing Norwich star Emi Buendia this month for a fee in the region of £20-25m.

Despite the recent involvement of academy graduate Emile Smith Rowe, manager Mikel Arteta is hoping to address his team’s lack of creativity in the transfer market, with Mesut Ozil shown the exit door.

Buendia is a favourable target for the Gunners as the playmaker would arrive with Premier League experience, yet Norwich would not command an excessive transfer fee.

Buendia’s fit at Arsenal
The Argentine created 3.1 chances per 90 minutes and registered seven assists in the Premier League last season, along with a key pass rate of 3.3 per 90 minutes, only behind Manchester City’s Kevin De Bruyne.

Buendia has then continued his impressive form into the Championship this season, having recorded seven goals and seven assists in his 19 appearances as the Canaries sit four points clear at the top.

As for his position, Buendia typically likes to drift in from the right side, allowing the full-back to push on and cross the ball if necessary. Otherwise, Buendia will find pockets of space in front of the opposition’s back line, making it a tough ask to mark him due to his fluid style.

However, with the emergence of Bukayo Saka on the right wing in recent weeks, Buendia may be brought in to play the number ’10’ role in a 4-2-3-1 formation which would allow more touches of the ball. Surrounded by the pace of Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, Saka and in-form Alexandre Lacazette, Buendia could play killer passes into the front three more frequently and with greater threat from central areas.

Although, Mikel Arteta has expressed his desire to play a 4-3-3, once he has the right personnel. This could see an attacking line-up with Buendia and Smith Rowe as ‘free 8’s’ either side of a defensive anchor such as Thomas Partey. In this instance, Buendia could alternate with Smith Rowe in dropping deep to receive the ball or pushing on into the attacking third.

Out of possession, Buendia’s aggression can lead to both yellow and red cards on occasion, but this bite is not always a negative. Buendia plays on the edge which, outside of full-back Kieran Tierney, is largely missing from the current Arsenal squad.

In addition, the Argentine can also be effective when engaging in the counter press. Buendia uses his speed to close down and force mistakes out of the opponent, both lifting the crowd and giving energy to his teammates. Buendia has an excellent work rate and appears to give everything to regain possession.

Norwich manager Daniel Farke remains confident of keeping his star man as his team bid to gain automatic promotion to the Premier League at the first time of asking.

“Emi is a key player for us, an important member of the group, and he is totally committed to us,” Farke said.

Buendia is not the Gunners’ only target, as they are also tracking Borussia Dortmund midfielder Julian Brandt, who has a rumored price tag of €25m.

1️⃣Buendia 在阿仙奴的引援名單上，不過並沒有處於最頂端。阿仙奴觀察他18個月了，欣賞他在英超和英冠的表現，但是槍手不會為他支付報導所說的4000萬鎊。諾域治很希望留住 Buendia，他是球隊沖超的關鍵人物。 我不認為阿仙奴會允許自家青訓球員如禾洛克或者 Nelson 作為籌碼永久轉會離開，外租倒是可以考慮。

4️⃣目前，阿迪達對史密夫路維的定位是成為另一個世界級十號位球員的備選和競爭者，在需要的時候挺身而出，在訓練中向世界級球員學習，最終接替這個世界級球員的位置。阿仙奴正在嘗試簽下一個世界級10號位球員。

終於有出路？奧斯爾有望一月離兵工廠

奧斯爾今季在陸仙奴可說毫無上陣機會，他的經理人Dr Erkut Sogut似乎一改之前強調奧斯爾會留效至合約夏季屆滿之口風，指該前德國腳有望在一月離隊。

經理人在一個跟ESPN的訪問中說：「1月1號之前我們不可合法地講，所以現在我們才剛開始研究不同選項。我不能直接講哪些球會，因為太不專業了，但總括而言，合約尚有6個月，我們跟不同人洽商不同機會，並嘗試為奧斯爾尋找最好的選擇，也並非不尋常。他可能留在阿仙奴到夏季，但也有可能離開，奧斯爾首選是留下來，但你知足球世界日新月異。」經理人亦指，轉會窗重開，情況會更明朗化。

有土耳其報道指，奧斯爾可能會去費倫巴治，也有傳聞指美職聯的華盛頓聯隊對他感興趣，甚至有消息指中東球會也有機會。(Goal.com)

阿迪達：外借沙利巴逼不得已

【Now Sports】阿仙奴領隊阿迪達指出，球會將會有不少球員於冬季轉會窗離開，除了奧斯爾有機會出「廠」之外，沙利巴亦會被外借。
阿迪達（Mikel Arteta）談到阿仙奴隊中的人事問題時，提到了奧斯爾與及上季外借聖伊天的沙利巴（William Saliba），後者將以借將身份效力法甲尼斯直到季尾。阿迪達表示外借是必需的：「他是我們的關鍵收購，但問題是沙利巴在過去18個月都沒有足夠的上陣時間。他現在的年紀正是關鍵發展階段，上陣時間是非常重要。給他3、4場比賽是不足夠的，他需要更多。我知很難去解釋，很難令人明白為何球會耗費金錢買他回來，但又同時外借他。要知道他身上發生了很多事，但沙利巴將會是己隊未來的重要成員，我們要好好保護他。」

至於奧斯爾的問題，阿迪達指出球會已「開綠燈」讓他與其他球會洽談：「未來數天會有決定吧，我暫時未知道會發生甚麼事。現在他可以自由跟其他球會討價還價，然後我們內部會作出討論，看看那一個才是最適合他的方案。每一位球員都想參與比賽，那亦是球會讓他們加盟的原因。對未能上場比賽的球員而言，時間一定過得很艱難。」

32歲的奧斯爾自3月起已未曾為阿仙奴上陣過，球隊今季更沒有為他註冊，不少傳媒估計他即將離隊他投。至於沙利巴則是2019年夏季來投，上季外借聖伊天時只上陣了12場比賽，上陣時間甚為不足。

圖片來源 : ©AFP

阿迪達為奧燒1月離隊設限

【Now Sports】阿仙奴領隊阿迪達回應奧斯爾離隊傳聞時指出，如果相關交易對球會及球員達成雙贏局面的話，將允許奧斯爾在冬季轉會窗離開。
外界盛傳奧斯爾（Mesut Ozil）獲土超費倫巴治、美職華盛頓聯賽斟介，而其經理人蘇古特亦承認正在接洽其他球會。阿仙奴領隊阿迪達（Mikel Arteta）回應相關傳聞時強調：「如果能夠在今個月進行一些調整，對球會和球員雙方都有好處。只要這是對奧斯爾和他的未來有利，同時也令球會有所得益，一切都會順利進行，不然球員就只能繼續留隊。」

奧斯爾離開阿仙奴似乎勢在必行，但離隊之前會否有機會在球迷面前再一次披起槍手戰袍，阿迪達指出：「我認為每個球員都渴望比賽，對於不獲上陣的球員當然難受。我們知道有一名過去幾季的主力球員在陣，但我必須決定哪些球員獲得上陣機會，我知道後果。我們會繼續平衡球會和球隊之間的利益，並為球員尋找正確的解決方案。」

阿仙奴在夏季轉會窗簽入里昂中場阿華（Houssem Aouar）不果，阿迪達與奧斯爾又陷入將帥不和，令到阿仙奴遲遲未能解決球會欠缺創造力的問題，預計阿迪達在今個轉會窗覓得新的進攻中場之後，才願意放走奧斯爾。

圖片來源 : ©AFP

鄺頌晴：港府為「防火牆」封網鋪路 要求網路供應商封鎖特定網站

流亡德國的鍵盤戰線前發言人鄺頌晴表示，香港自周二起無法讀過特定網路供應商 （ISP）Smartone、CMHK、HKBN及PCCW造訪特定網站。經實驗後發現只能透過VPN才能在香港境內造訪「香港編年史」。 同時，她亦收到在CSL和Smartone工作的員工透露，政府正要求香港（ISP）封鎖特定網站 （Cite blocking)。
